[[File:Chapter-39-Fig.-6b.svg|thumb|Referenced here <ref>https://www.meaningcrisis.co/ep-39-awakening-from-the-meaning-crisis-the-religion-of-no-religion/</ref><ref>[[Reacting to the Meaning Crisis]] episode 39</ref><ref>[[Reacting to the Meaning Crisis]] episode 40 begins around 25:00-32:21</ref>]]
Desire synthesis is the process by which a [[sapient]] synthesizes its desires together in order to build a [[narrative]] it lives its life based on.

It can occur in both collective groups of sapients and individual minds. It is unclear whether this is a necessary feature of sapients or whether one could have language without desire synthesis or desire synthesis without language

Desire synthesis likely uses some kind of [[Servitors]]

Human male promiscuity may be a product of [[incomplete desire synthesis]].

== Misc references ==
<ref>https://youtu.be/jbziEOT2V4c?t=2918</ref>

Discussion here<ref>[[Reacting to the Meaning Crisis]] episode 41 2:16</ref>

== References ==
[[Category:Sapient Psychology]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Desire synthesis]][[sn:Desire synthesis]]<references />
[[Category:Pages]]
